DISCOVERY 610, PLAINTIFF'S MOTIONS TO: COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AND FOR SANCTIONS -- RUSSELL DAVIS APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY. ELIZABETH WILLIAMS APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT. ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: THE COURT CONSIDERED THE LATE FILED OPPOSITION, PLAINTIFF'S MOTIONS PLAINTIFF'S MOTIONS TO: COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS ARE GRANTED. DEFENDANTS ANA MILENEWICZ AND MILLENIUM 2000 MILPITAS REALTY INC. ARE ORDERED TO PROVIDE COMPLETE RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTION WITHIN 14 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO DEEM CERTAIN MATTERS ADMITTED IS GRANTED. FAILURE TO RESPOND WAS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $858.90, TO BE PAID WITHIN 14 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN

DISCOVERY 610, APPEARANCES: RUSSELL DAVIS FOR PLAINTIFF AND OPPOSING PARTY: H. HERNANDEZ AND N. BETANCOURT NO OTHER APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: 1) DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM TERMS OF DISCOVERY ORDER: A) MONETARY SANCTIONS THE COURT WILL CONSIDER THE MOTION TO RECONSIDER THE PREVIOUS AWARD OF MONETARY SANCTIONS AS BROUGHT PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 1008. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS UNTIMELY. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT APPEARED ON JANUARY 24, 2005 WITH AN EX PARTE APPLICATION TO FILE THE INSTANT MOTION SEEKING RELIEF FROM THE JANUARY 3, 2005 ORDER GRANTING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES, TO DEEM CERTAIN MATTERS ADMITTED, AND TO AWARD MONETARY SANCTIONS. AS A COPY OF THE ORDER WAS SERVED ON DEFENDANT ON JANUARY 3, 2005, THE MOTION IS UNTIMELY PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 1008 (A) AND (E). B) REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR RELIEF RELATING TO THE JANUARY 3, 2005 ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 2033(K), HAVING BEEN BROUGHT AFTER THE ISSUANCE OF THE ORDER TO DEEM CERTAIN MATTERS ADMITTED, IS UNTIMELY AND THEREFORE DENIED. THE COURT PREVIOUSLY READ AND CONSIDERED DEFENDANT'S LATE-FILED OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL AND FOUND THAT DEFENDANT HAD NOT SERVED RESPONSES TO THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION THAT SUBSTANTIALLY COMPLIED WITH THE REQUIREMENTS OF CCP SECTION 2033(F)(1) BEFORE THE HEARING ON JANUARY 3, 2005. ACCORDINGLY, DEFENDANT IS NOT ENTITLED TO RELIEF PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 2033(K). C) PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FORM INTERROGATORIES DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR RELIEF RELATING TO THE JANUARY 3, 2005 ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 2030(K) AND 2031(L), HAVING BEEN BROUGHT AFTER THE ISSUANCE OF THE ORDER TO COMPEL PRODUCTION, IS UNTIMELY AND THEREFORE DENIED. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

DISCOVERY 610, APPEARANCES: RUSSELL DAVIS FOR PLAINTIFF AND MOVING PARTY: HELADIO HERNANDEZ AND NORMA BETANCOURT NO OTHER APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH THE PREVIOUS COURT ORDER: THE MOTION IS MOOT AS DEFENDANT SERVED RESPONSES TO THE REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FORM INTERROGATORIES ON JANUARY 27, 2005. THE COURT MAKES NO FINDINGS AS TO THE ADEQUACY OF SAID RESPONSES. FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE PRIOR ORDER BY NOT SERVING THE RESPONSES IN A TIMELY FASHION WAS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $661.30 ARE TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
